title: leucanthemum corymbosum
intro: Leucanthemum corymbosum, also known as the Shasta daisy, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Leucanthemum corymbosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the flowers or leaves in hot water.
